Shelagh Delaney, acclaimed playwright whose ground-breaking debut, Taste Honey, challenged many taboos Britain, died.
been suffering from cancer.
feisty Salford-born writer, drew gritty reality working-class life, pioneers "kitchen sink" realism movement late 1950s 1960s.
just when Taste Honey premiered 1958 became instant success, with runs London York.
earthy moving story teenage girl's pregnancy following one-night stand with black sailor, supportive relationship with artist, would become defining feminist plays 1950s.
subsequently turned into film, starring Rita Tushingham earning Delaney film's director, Tony Richardson, Bafta Writer's Guild awards best screenplay.
Tushingham told Guardian: "Shelagh such important person start career.
deeply saddened that have lost such amazing talent."
Jane Villiers, Delaney's agent last years, told Channel word wasted.
Every word meant something.
extraordinarily accurate about characters."
daughter inspector Irish descent, Delaney wrote Taste Honey, aged just after seeing disliking Terence Rattigan's play Variation Theme, partly because felt work showed insensitivity portrayed homosexuality.
wrote weeks, adapting from novel already working
second play, Lion Love, which portrayed impoverished family difficult marriage, enjoy same critical success when opened 1960.
write theatre again until 1979, when revised series House that Jack Built.
between wrote screenplays: White (1965), Charlie Bubbles (1967), Raging Moon (1970), screenplay 1985 film Dance with Stranger, based story Ruth Ellis, last woman executed crime Britain.
Driven, part, rebellion against theatre which, claimed, portray "life majority ordinary people know it", found vitality Salford that infused writing.
"The language alive, it's virile, lives breathes know exactly where it's coming from.
Right earth," said 1960 film Russell.
"Down river it's even romantic, stand smell."
Acknowledging inspiration, author Jeanette Winterson said Delaney's talent been allowed fade because gender.
"She like lighthouse pointing warning about rocks underneath.
first working-class woman playwright," Winterson wrote Guardian last year.
"She talent go."
singer Morrissey frequently referenced writing lyrics, particularly song This Night Opened Eyes, face appeared cover Smiths' compilation album Louder Than Bombs single Girlfriend Coma.
Beatles were also said fans, recording their version theme from film adaptation Taste Honey.
